rationale.R1.2: A review of the section centrally involves checking whether the argumentative progression from method to results to broad claims is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section reports quantitative results and uses them to support claims about effectiveness, so claim-evidence alignment is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes state-of-the-art and new-standard claims, making novelty assessment central to the requested review.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: The section relies on named climate references and scenario backbones, so citation accuracy and scholarly grounding are central.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what can be judged from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility includes detecting and avoiding unsupported broad claims in the section.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection rather than performing the requested review.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns climate decision-support workflows, downstream-harm avoidance remains active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit features.
